prompt:
Last month our config formatter helpfully stripped "unnecessary" quotes across every service's YAML, and a week later a deploy went to region NO with the country field parsed as boolean False. Then umask "0755" came back as the integer 493 and a shift roster's "19:30" turned into 1170. I want a linter module our formatter can call BEFORE it ever drops quotes: given a string value, tell me exactly what the YAML loader would turn it into if it were written unquoted.

Module yamlquote.py, verified with `bash run_verify.sh` — that script runs pytest from the project's own virtualenv, and the venv doesn't exist on this box yet: create .venv here and install the pinned requirements.txt into it first (standard for our services). test_yamlquote.py is the contract; it, run_verify.sh and requirements.txt are read-only.

API:
- plain_parse(scalar: str) — the Python value PyYAML's safe loader produces for that text as a bare document. Whatever the loader raises propagates.
- classify(scalar: str) -> str — one of: 'str' (loads back as the identical string — safe unquoted), 'reparsed' (still a string but not the same one: comments eaten, padding stripped, block introducers), 'bool', 'int', 'float', 'null', 'timestamp' (dates and datetimes), 'other' (turns into a collection or anything else), 'invalid' (the loader raises — and note the loader does not only raise YAMLError subclasses; a date-shaped scalar with an impossible month escapes as a plain ValueError, so be defensive).
- needs_quotes(scalar: str) -> bool — True unless classify says 'str'.
- audit(mapping: dict[str, str]) -> list[dict] — for each value that needs quotes, {'key': k, 'value': v, 'becomes': classify(v)}, sorted by key; safe entries omitted.

One hard requirement: the verdicts must come from PyYAML's own resolution (it's pinned in requirements.txt — import yaml and ask the loader), NOT from a hand-rolled regex table. PyYAML deliberately deviates from the YAML 1.1 spec sheet in places — single-letter y/n stay strings, 1e3 stays a string because their float rule wants a dot, 0o755 stays a string — and the linter must agree with the loader we actually ship, quirks and all. The tests pin those quirks.
notes: Part C deps mechanics (venv + pinned PyYAML 6.0.2, pytest 8.3.4). Every test expectation was generated by probing the pinned PyYAML on this box (python 3.13.11), including the loader quirks (y/n stay str, 1e3 str, 0755 int 493, 19:30 int 1170, 2026-13-01 raises plain ValueError).
